title: perilla citriodora
intro: Perilla citriodora, also known as Korean perilla or wild basil, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to Asia, valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The plant's leaves and seeds are rich in antioxidants and have been traditionally used to alleviate various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory issues, offering benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Perilla citriodora can be prepared in various forms, including tea, oil, capsules, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
